method: Held-out predictability of the document TOKEN-TYPE grammar (L2/L3, token TYPES only: word/num/nl/div/other; NO sign identities, NO numeral values, NO phonetics, NO meaning). First-order MARKOV model P(next_type|cur_type), Laplace-smoothed (add-1 over the 7-symbol vocab incl. BOS/EOS), trained on TRAIN inscriptions ([BOS]+types+[EOS]); UNIGRAM baseline = marginal type frequencies incl. BOS/EOS, Laplace-smoothed. Held-out cross-entropy xent=-mean log2 P(t_i|context) bits/token over all non-BOS tokens (EOS counted); IMPROVEMENT = unigram_xent - markov_xent (>0 => Markov grammar predictive). Two splits: (a) RANDOM 5-fold CV (seed-frozen); (b) SITE-BLOCKED (train on all-but-one site, test on held-out site, for each site with >=15 inscriptions). Significance via order-shuffle permutation null (>=200 shuffles of TEST interior types, BOS/EOS fixed): real Markov xent must beat shuffled. POSITIVE CONTROL gates verdict: (a) DETECT planted word->num->nl grammar (improvement>0, perm p<=0.05); (b) FALSE-POSITIVE on order-shuffled streams (improvement~=0, not significant across >=20 sets).
result: PC PASSED (synthetic planted word->num->nl grammar DETECTED: mean improvement +1.501 bits/token, mean perm p=0.005; FALSE-POSITIVE control clean: order-shuffled streams show mean improvement +0.089 bits/token with 0/20 sets significant). GLOBAL LA: n=1341 inscriptions. RANDOM 5-fold CV: Markov xent ~1.53 bits/token vs unigram ~2.56 bits/token => mean IMPROVEMENT +1.014 bits/token, mean perm p=0.005 (significant). SITE-BLOCKED: 9 sites qualify (>=15 inscriptions); ALL 9/9 sites show held-out improvement>0 with perm p=0.005 (Haghia Triada +1.030, Khania +0.880, Knossos +0.769, Malia +1.127, Palaikastro +1.024, Phaistos +0.771, Thera +0.707, Zakros +0.961, Iouktas +0.970). The token-TYPE transition grammar is PREDICTIVE on held-out inscriptions AND GENERALIZES across unseen sites (>=3 required, 9 achieved). Verdict: DOC_GRAMMAR_PREDICTIVE_HELDOUT.
key_findings: The Linear A document TOKEN-TYPE stream has a PREDICTABLE first-order grammar that GENERALIZES to held-out inscriptions: random 5-fold CV gives a mean improvement of +1.014 bits/token of Markov over unigram (Markov xent ~1.53 vs unigram ~2.56 bits/token), significant by the order-shuffle permutation null (mean p=0.005). Shuffling the interior token order destroys the predictability, confirming the gain is genuinely sequential.

The grammar GENERALIZES ACROSS UNSEEN SITES: in the site-blocked split (train on all-but-one site, test on the held-out site), ALL 9 qualifying sites (>=15 inscriptions) show held-out improvement>0 with perm p=0.005 — Haghia Triada +1.030, Khania +0.880, Knossos +0.769, Malia +1.127, Palaikastro +1.024, Phaistos +0.771, Thera +0.707, Zakros +0.961, Iouktas +0.970 bits/token. The token-type transition structure is shared across the Linear A scribal tradition, not site-local.

POSITIVE CONTROL PASSED on both gates: the machinery DETECTS a planted word->num->nl transition grammar (mean improvement +1.501 bits/token, perm p=0.005) and produces NO false positives on order-shuffled streams (mean improvement +0.089 bits/token, 0/20 sets significant). The ~1 bit/token LA improvement is far above the shuffled null, so the result is not a machinery artifact.

The magnitude (~1 bit/token, roughly 40% reduction in per-token uncertainty vs the unigram baseline) is large and remarkably uniform across folds and sites, indicating a strong, stable document-level regularity in the ordering of token TYPES (e.g. word/numeral/newline structure) — consistent with the certified E031 (word->numeral) and E037 (numeral-line-final) findings, now consolidated into a single predictive held-out test.

This is the closest L2/L3 analog to 'predicts held-out material': a model trained on one set of inscriptions predicts the token-TYPE ordering of unseen inscriptions and unseen sites significantly better than a context-free baseline. No sign identities, numeral values, phonetics, or meaning were used.
successor_hypotheses: H1 (L2/L3): Fit a SECOND-ORDER Markov model over token types and test whether it beats the first-order model on held-out cross-entropy — this would quantify how much additional structure lives in 2-step dependencies (e.g. word->num->nl chains) beyond the first-order transitions, still using token TYPES only.

H2 (L2/L3): Extract and rank the highest-information token-TYPE transitions (e.g. the strongest P(num|word), P(nl|num), P(EOS|nl)) from the trained matrix and characterize the implied document 'skeleton' (token-type grammar only, no sign identities).

H3 (L2/L3): Test whether the token-type grammar DIFFERS by inscription class/support (tablet vs seal vs other) — train a per-class Markov model and compare held-out cross-entropy against the pooled model (token types only).

H4 (L2/L3): Build the synthetic LB token-type control stream and compare its Markov-vs-unigram improvement to LA's, to test whether the document token-type grammar strength is distinctive to LA or a generic administrative-text property (LB control-only, synthetic stream explicitly labelled).

H5 (L2/L3): Quantify the CONTRIBUTION of each token TYPE to the predictability gain via leave-one-type-out ablation (collapse one type into 'other', retrain, re-evaluate held-out xent) — identifies which token classes carry the sequential structure (token types only).

H6 (L2/L3): Test predictability of token-type grammar as a function of inscription LENGTH — do short inscriptions obey the same transition grammar as long ones, or is the grammar length-conditional? (token types only, length-binned held-out evaluation).
non_circular: Pure L2/L3 token-TYPE sequence statistics on the ORDERED per-inscription stream of token TYPES (word/num/nl/div/other). NO sign identities, NO numeral values, NO phonetics, NO sound, NO meaning, NO reading. Only the TYPE label of each token and its position. LB has no native token-type stream; a synthetic LB control is specified in the machinery but the verdict rests entirely on LA held-out cross-entropy. Verdict is mechanical from the frozen rule (PC passed AND global CV improvement>0 significant AND site-blocked held-out improvement>0 in >=3 sites). L2/L3 only.
deviations: No deviations from the frozen prereg/plan. The machinery was run exactly as specified (frozen seeds, Laplace add-1 smoothing, 5-fold CV, site-blocked with MIN_SITE_N=15, order-shuffle permutation null with 200 shuffles, 20 false-positive control sets). result.json and EPOCH047_REPORT.md were generated in this repair pass because they were missing from the initial epoch output; prereg.md and plan_hash.txt were NOT modified.
